documentation.readme: 🔐 Hack WiFi AI - Complete WiFi Password Cracking Solution in 2026
usage_sections: WPA/WPA2 Cracking: 85% success rate on weak passwords
AI Efficiency: 3x faster than traditional brute force
Real Handshake Capture: Actual WPA handshake interception
🎣 Real Handshake Capture — Captures actual WPA 4-way handshakes

arguments.name: --bssid
arguments.description: Target BSSID (MAC of an access point).

arguments.name: --channel
arguments.description: WiFi channel to operate on (1-165).

arguments.name: --max-delay
arguments.description: CLI flag '--max-delay' accepted by the tool; behavior is implementation-defined and should be confirmed against the upstream README.

arguments.name: --min-delay
arguments.description: CLI flag '--min-delay' accepted by the tool; behavior is implementation-defined and should be confirmed against the upstream README.

arguments.name: --stealth
arguments.description: Enable stealth / passive mode (slower, fewer artifacts).

arguments.name: --timeout
arguments.description: Per-operation timeout in seconds.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --port
arguments.description: Target port (default: service default).

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable for higher levels).

arguments.name: --threads
arguments.description: Number of concurrent workers.

arguments.name: --rate
arguments.description: Packet / request rate limit (packets/sec).

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP or SOCKS proxy URL for outbound requests.

arguments.name: --user-agent
arguments.description: HTTP User-Agent header value.

arguments.name: --cookie
arguments.description: HTTP Cookie header value.

arguments.name: --header
arguments.description: Extra HTTP header in key:value form (repeatable).

arguments.name: --insecure
arguments.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (lab use only).

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Emit JSON output instead of human-readable text.

arguments.name: --no-color
arguments.description: Disable ANSI color in output.

arguments.name: --quiet
arguments.description: Suppress non-error output.

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --yes
arguments.description: Skip confirmation prompts (use with care).

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [wifi_2_4_ghz, wifi_5_ghz, shell_linux] in the Wireless category. Fits when the engagement needs: 🔐 Hack WiFi AI - Complete WiFi Password Cracking Solution in 2026.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: 🔐 Hack WiFi AI - Complete WiFi Password Cracking Solution in 2026.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: git clone https://github.com/HoffmannAlex/Hack-WiFi-Password-With-AI/hack-wifi-ai.git. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `sudo ./install.sh`. Further invocations from upstream: `sudo python main.py`; `sudo chmod +x install.sh && sudo ./install.sh`. 🔐 Hack WiFi AI - Complete WiFi Password Cracking Solution in 2026.
